section.heading: Special boards of inquiry on air transportation safety

subsection.heading: Establishment.—
subsection.chapeau: If an accident involves a substantial question about public safety in air transportation, the National Transportation Safety Board may establish a special board of inquiry composed of—

paragraph.content: one member of the Board acting as chairman; and
2 members representing the public, appointed by the President on notification of the establishment of the special board of inquiry.

subsection.heading: Qualifications and Conflicts of Interest.—
subsection.content: The public members of a special board of inquiry must be qualified by training and experience to participate in the inquiry and may not have a pecuniary interest in an aviation enterprise involved in the accident to be investigated.

subsection.heading: Authority.—
subsection.content: A special board of inquiry has the same authority that the Board has under this chapter.

note.p: In subsection (c), the words “when convened to investigate an accident certified to it by the National Transportation Safety Board” are omitted as surplus.
